hypothesis: If adaptive, multi-metric diversity constraints from NaviDiv that penalize overrepresented structures are applied during REINVENT4 singlet-fission optimization, then the generated molecules will exhibit significantly higher chemical diversity (e.g., lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, and higher unique fragment/scaffold percentages) without a decrease in ΔE_SF scoring performance, because dynamic penalties steer the policy away from mode collapse while preserving property-driven reward optimization.
